Hoffbrand — Chapter 2: Erythropoiesis and general aspects of anaemia

20 single best answer (SBA) questions · Expert level

Question 11 of 2020 total
Question 11

A 28-year-old man is found to have a haemoglobin of 195 g/L during a routine insurance medical examination. He is a non-smoker with no cardiopulmonary disease. His serum erythropoietin level is inappropriately normal. His father had a similar erythrocytosis. Which of the following molecular defects is most likely responsible for his condition?
